 # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 clear
 echo -e "${CYAN}"
 echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
 echo "║              PvPBot - Build Pipeline                ║"
 echo "╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"
 printf "║ %-25s : %-25s ║\n" "Target Server" "$TARGET_SERVER"
 printf "║ %-25s : %-25s ║\n" "Platforms" "$SUPPORTED_PLATFORMS"
 printf "║ %-25s : %-25s ║\n" "Required Java" "$REQUIRED_JAVA"
 printf "║ %-25s : %-25s ║\n" "Project Type" "$PROJECT_TYPE"
 printf "║ %-25s : %-25s ║\n" "Working Dir" "$(pwd)"
 echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
 echo -e "${NC}"
 
 # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 #   STEP 1: CLEAN
 # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 step_header "[1/4] Cleaning build cache..."
 
 ./gradlew clean
 [ $? -ne 0 ] && abort "Clean failed!"
 echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Clean complete.${NC}"
 
 mkdir -p build
 echo "PVPBOT BUILD REPORT - $(date)" > "$REPORT_FILE"
 echo "=========================================" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
 echo "Step 1: Clean -> OK" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
 
 # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 #   STEP 2: TESTS & STATIC ANALYSIS
 # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 step_header "[2/4] Running unit tests & static analysis..."
 
 # 2a: Unit tests
 ./gradlew test
 TEST_EXIT=$?
 echo "Step 2a: Test -> $([ $TEST_EXIT -eq 0 ] && echo 'OK' || echo 'FAILED')" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
 if [ $TEST_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
     abort "Unit tests failed!"
 fi
 echo -e "${GREEN}✓ All tests passed.${NC}"
 
 # 2b: Static security & performance scans
 WARN_COUNT=0
 
 echo -e "${YELLOW}→ Checking synchronous network calls...${NC}"
 SYNC_HTTP=$(grep -rn -e "HttpURLConnection" -e "HttpClient" "$SRC_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true)
 if [ -n "$SYNC_HTTP" ]; then
     echo -e "${RED}[WARN] Synchronous HTTP calls detected:${NC}"
     echo "$SYNC_HTTP"
     echo -e "[WARN] Synchronous network calls:\n$SYNC_HTTP" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
     WARN_COUNT=$((WARN_COUNT + 1))
 else
     echo -e "${GREEN}  ✓ No blocking network calls.${NC}"
 fi
 
 echo -e "${YELLOW}→ Checking command permission gates...${NC}"
 MISSING_PERMS=""
 while IFS= read -r line; do
     MISSING_PERMS="${MISSING_PERMS}  - ${line}"$'\n'
 done < <(grep -rn "onCommand" "$SRC_DIR" 2>/dev/null \
     | grep -v "hasPermission" 2>/dev/null \
     | grep -v "public boolean onCommand" 2>/dev/null \
     | grep -v "implements" 2>/dev/null \
     | grep -v "/*" 2>/dev/null \
     | grep -v "*" 2>/dev/null \
     || true)
 if [ -n "$MISSING_PERMS" ]; then
     echo -e "${RED}[WARN] Commands without hasPermission():${NC}"
     echo -e "$MISSING_PERMS"
     echo -e "[WARN] Missing permission checks:" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
     echo -e "$MISSING_PERMS" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
     WARN_COUNT=$((WARN_COUNT + 1))
 else
     echo -e "${GREEN}  ✓ All commands have permission gates.${NC}"
 fi
 
 echo -e "${YELLOW}→ Checking unsafe Java reflection...${NC}"
 UNSAFE_REFLECT=$(grep -rn -e "setAccessible(true)" -e "Class.forName" "$SRC_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true)
 if [ -n "$UNSAFE_REFLECT" ]; then
     echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN] Raw reflection usage:${NC}"
     echo "$UNSAFE_REFLECT"
     echo -e "[WARN] Unsafe reflection:\n$UNSAFE_REFLECT" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
     WARN_COUNT=$((WARN_COUNT + 1))
 else
     echo -e "${GREEN}  ✓ No unsafe reflection.${NC}"
 fi
 
 echo -e "${YELLOW}→ Checking Thread.sleep usage...${NC}"
 THREAD_SLEEP=$(grep -rn "Thread\.sleep" "$SRC_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true)
 if [ -n "$THREAD_SLEEP" ]; then
     echo -e "${RED}[CRITICAL] Thread.sleep detected (blocks main thread):${NC}"
     echo "$THREAD_SLEEP"
     echo -e "[CRITICAL] Thread.sleep:\n$THREAD_SLEEP" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
     WARN_COUNT=$((WARN_COUNT + 1))
 else
     echo -e "${GREEN}  ✓ No Thread.sleep found.${NC}"
 fi
 
 echo "Warnings: $WARN_COUNT" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
 echo "Step 2: Scan -> OK ($WARN_COUNT warnings)" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
 
 # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 #   STEP 3: BUILD
 # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 step_header "[3/4] Compiling & reobfuscating production jar..."
 
 ./gradlew build
 BUILD_EXIT=$?
 echo "Step 3: Build -> $([ $BUILD_EXIT -eq 0 ] && echo 'OK' || echo 'FAILED')" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
 if [ $BUILD_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
     abort "Build failed!"
 fi
 echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Build successful.${NC}"
 ls -lh build/libs/*.jar 2>/dev/null
 ls -lh build/libs/*.jar >> "$REPORT_FILE" 2>/dev/null
 
 # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 #   STEP 4: CLEAN UP TEMPORARY BUILD ARTIFACTS
 # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 step_header "[4/4] Cleaning up temporary build artifacts..."
 
 for dir in build/tmp build/classes build/generated build/reports build/generated-sources; do
     if [ -d "$dir" ]; then
         rm -rf "$dir"
         echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓ Removed${NC} $dir"
     fi
 done
 
 echo "Step 4: Cleanup -> OK" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
 echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Temporary artifacts cleaned.${NC}"
 
 # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 #   SUMMARY
 # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 echo ""
 echo -e "${CYAN}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗${NC}"
 if [ $WARN_COUNT -gt 0 ]; then
     echo -e "${YELLOW}║   Pipeline finished with $WARN_COUNT warning(s)         ║${NC}"
     echo -e "${YELLOW}║   See: $REPORT_FILE            ║${NC}"
 else
     echo -e "${GREEN}║   Pipeline completed — 100% clean                      ║${NC}"
 fi
 echo -e "${CYAN}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝${NC}"
 
 exit 0

Role: Agent A2 (Lead Coder)
Task: Phase 1 - Advanced Project Rebuild & Citizens Base Framework (Java 25 / Paper 1.21.11)

---

### ⚠️ IMPORTANT ARCHITECTURAL DECISIONS (MANDATORY):
1. **SELECT OPTION (A):** Delete the entire old `src/` directory immediately and rebuild the project cleanly from scratch to eliminate all handwritten NMS connection/packet listener legacy files.
2. **ENVIRONMENT:** Target strictly **Java 25 (LTS)** on **Paper 1.21.11**.
3. **LIBRARIES:** Integrate **Citizens2** (for NPC presence, network pipelines, and world tracking) and **ProtocolLib** (for smooth rotation packets).

---

### PHASE 1 - FILE SPECIFICATIONS & REQUIREMENTS:

Please create and fully implement the following 8 core files. Do not use any placeholders, abbreviations, or `// TODO` blocks.

#### 1. Build File: `build.gradle` (Groovy DSL)
Configure a clean, modern Groovy Gradle build script:
- Set compiler toolchain and compatibility settings strictly to Java 25.
- Add repositories: `mavenCentral()`, PaperMC public repository, and the Citizens repository (`https://repo.citizensnpcs.co/`).
- Declare dependencies (compileOnly):
  - `paperDevBundle("1.21.11-R0.1-SNAPSHOT")`
  - `net.citizensnpcs:citizens-api:2.0.35-SNAPSHOT`
  - `com.comphenix.protocol:ProtocolLib:5.5.0-SNAPSHOT`

#### 2. Settings Registry: `src/main/java/com/pvpbot/bot/BotSettings.java`
Create the comprehensive settings container supporting all original Modrinth settings commands. Must include:
- Private fields with default values:
  - Movement: `moveSpeed` (double, default 0.25), `bhop` (boolean, default false), `idle` (boolean, default false), `idleRadius` (int, default 15).
  - Combat: `combat` (boolean, default true), `revenge` (boolean, default true), `autoTarget` (boolean, default true), `criticals` (boolean, default true).
  - Targets: `targetPlayers` (boolean, default true), `targetMobs` (boolean, default false), `targetBots` (boolean, default true).
  - Melee/Ranged/Mace: `attackCooldown` (int, default 12), `meleeRange` (double, default 3.0), `preferSword` (boolean, default true), `ranged` (boolean, default true), `mace` (boolean, default true).
  - Ranged parameters: `rangedMinRange` (double, default 3.0), `rangedOptimalRange` (double, default 10.0), `rangedMaxRange` (double, default 20.0), `bowDrawTicks` (int, default 20), `arrowPrediction` (boolean, default true), `rangedStrafe` (boolean, default true), `rangedRetreat` (boolean, default true).
  - Shield: `autoShield` (boolean, default true), `shieldBreak` (boolean, default true), `shieldBreakChance` (int, default 30), `shieldHoldTicks` (int, default 60), `shieldRaiseTicks` (int, default 5), `shieldMace` (boolean, default true).
  - Survival: `autoArmor` (boolean, default true), `autoWeapon` (boolean, default true), `autoEat` (boolean, default true), `autoPotion` (boolean, default true), `autoMend` (boolean, default true), `autoTotem` (boolean, default true), `totemPriority` (boolean, default false).
  - Realism: `missChance` (int, default 5), `mistakeChance` (int, default 5), `aimSpeed` (double, default 15.0).
  - Lifecycle: `showInTab` (boolean, default true), `botLeaveOnDeath` (boolean, default false).
- A `loadFromConfig(FileConfiguration config)` method to parse values safely.
- A `saveToConfig(FileConfiguration config)` method to write values back cleanly.
- Thread-safe getters/setters for all fields with clamping logic for numeric bounds.

#### 3. Citizens Trait Class: `src/main/java/com/pvpbot/npc/PvPBotTrait.java`
Create the custom Citizens Trait to act as the core ticking/AI loop for our bot:
- Extends `net.citizensnpcs.api.trait.Trait`.
- Constructor: Calls `super("pvpbot");`.
- In `onAttach()`: Instantiate a local `BotSettings` for the NPC and load its configurations.
- In `run()` (Ticked 20 times/second by Citizens):
  - Ensure the NPC is spawned: `if (!npc.isSpawned()) return;`
  - Get the Bukkit Player entity safely: `org.bukkit.entity.Player player = (org.bukkit.entity.Player) npc.getEntity();`
  - Retrieve the NMS `ServerPlayer` instance:
    `net.minecraft.server.level.ServerPlayer nmsPlayer = ((org.bukkit.craftbukkit.entity.CraftPlayer) player).getHandle();`
  - Implement a basic safe tick logging mechanism (throttled to print once every 100 ticks via the plugin's logger) to verify the NMS tick loop is running successfully on the Citizens entity.
- Getter/setter for the local `BotSettings` instance.

#### 4. Lifecycle Manager: `src/main/java/com/pvpbot/bot/BotManager.java`
Create the bot manager using the Citizens API to spawn, locate, and clean up bots:
- Maintain a thread-safe registry: `Map<UUID, net.citizensnpcs.api.npc.NPC> activeNPCs = new java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap<>();`.
- `spawnBot(org.bukkit.Location loc, String name)`:
  - Retrieve Citizens registry: `net.citizensnpcs.api.npc.NPCRegistry registry = net.citizensnpcs.api.CitizensAPI.getNPCRegistry();`.
  - Create the PLAYER type NPC: `NPC npc = registry.createNPC(org.bukkit.entity.EntityType.PLAYER, name);`.
  - Add our `PvPBotTrait`: `npc.addTrait(PvPBotTrait.class);`.
  - Fetch default config parameters and apply them to the NPC's `PvPBotTrait` settings.
  - Spawn the NPC: `npc.spawn(loc);`.
  - Tab List visibility: If `showInTab` is true, let the bot remain listed. If false, schedule a task to send a removal packet 40 ticks later.
  - Register in the `activeNPCs` map.
- `removeBot(String name)`: Locate the NPC by name, and safely despawn and destroy it: `npc.destroy();` (which unregisters it and removes it from the world cleanly).
- `removeAll()`: Safe cleanup of all spawned NPCs on plugin reload/shutdown.

#### 5. Main Plugin Class: `src/main/java/com/pvpbot/PvPBotPlugin.java`
- Extends `org.bukkit.plugin.java.JavaPlugin`.
- Initialize `BotManager` and save/load default `config.yml`.
- Register the `/pvpbot` command.
- On Disable: Call `botManager.removeAll()` to prevent ghost NPC leaks in world files.

#### 6. Tab Executor Command: `src/main/java/com/pvpbot/command/PvPBotCommand.java`
- Implements `org.bukkit.command.TabExecutor`.
- Subcommands:
  - `/pvpbot spawn [name]` - Spawns a Citizens-backed bot at the player's current location.
  - `/pvpbot remove <name>` - Safely removes/destroys the bot.
  - `/pvpbot removeall` - Safely purges all spawned bots.
- Implements standard tab completion.

#### 7. Default Configurations (`src/main/resources/config.yml`):
Define default config keys matching `BotSettings` variables under `bot-settings` node.

#### 8. Plugin metadata (`src/main/resources/plugin.yml`):
Standard Paper plugin description declaring the main class, version, api-version: '1.21', dependencies: [ProtocolLib, Citizens], and the `/pvpbot` command.

---

### OUTPUT WORKFLOW & REPORT.MD:

#!/bin/bash

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#   PvPBot - Build Pipeline Script
#   Pipeline: clean → test/scan → build → cleanup
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ── Metadata Configuration ─────────────────────────────────
TARGET_SERVER="Paper 1.21.11"
SUPPORTED_PLATFORMS="Paper, Purpur (1.21.x)"
REQUIRED_JAVA="Java 21"
PROJECT_TYPE="Pure NMS PvP Bot Engine"
SRC_DIR="src/main/java"
REPORT_FILE="build/pipeline-report.txt"

# ── Colors ────────────────────────────────────────────────
GREEN='\033[1;32m'
RED='\033[1;31m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[1;34m'
CYAN='\033[1;36m'
NC='\033[0m'

# ── Environment ──────────────────────────────────────────
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-21-openjdk
export PATH=$JAVA_HOME/bin:$PATH

# ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────
abort() {
    echo -e "\n${RED}❌ $1${NC}"
    exit 1
}

step_header() {
    echo -e "\n${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    echo -e "${BLUE}  $1${NC}"
    echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
}

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#   DASHBOARD
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
clear
echo -e "${CYAN}"
echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║              PvPBot - Build Pipeline                ║"
echo "╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"
printf "║ %-25s : %-25s ║\n" "Target Server" "$TARGET_SERVER"
printf "║ %-25s : %-25s ║\n" "Platforms" "$SUPPORTED_PLATFORMS"
printf "║ %-25s : %-25s ║\n" "Required Java" "$REQUIRED_JAVA"
printf "║ %-25s : %-25s ║\n" "Project Type" "$PROJECT_TYPE"
printf "║ %-25s : %-25s ║\n" "Working Dir" "$(pwd)"
echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo -e "${NC}"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#   STEP 1: CLEAN
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
step_header "[1/4] Cleaning build cache..."

./gradlew clean
[ $? -ne 0 ] && abort "Clean failed!"
echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Clean complete.${NC}"

mkdir -p build
echo "PVPBOT BUILD REPORT - $(date)" > "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "=========================================" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "Step 1: Clean -> OK" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#   STEP 2: TESTS & STATIC ANALYSIS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
step_header "[2/4] Running unit tests & static analysis..."

# 2a: Unit tests
./gradlew test
TEST_EXIT=$?
echo "Step 2a: Test -> $([ $TEST_EXIT -eq 0 ] && echo 'OK' || echo 'FAILED')" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
if [ $TEST_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
    abort "Unit tests failed!"
fi
echo -e "${GREEN}✓ All tests passed.${NC}"

# 2b: Static security & performance scans
WARN_COUNT=0

echo -e "${YELLOW}→ Checking synchronous network calls...${NC}"
SYNC_HTTP=$(grep -rn -e "HttpURLConnection" -e "HttpClient" "$SRC_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true)
if [ -n "$SYNC_HTTP" ]; then
    echo -e "${RED}[WARN] Synchronous HTTP calls detected:${NC}"
    echo "$SYNC_HTTP"
    echo -e "[WARN] Synchronous network calls:\n$SYNC_HTTP" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
    WARN_COUNT=$((WARN_COUNT + 1))
else
    echo -e "${GREEN}  ✓ No blocking network calls.${NC}"
fi

echo -e "${YELLOW}→ Checking command permission gates...${NC}"
MISSING_PERMS=""
while IFS= read -r line; do
    MISSING_PERMS="${MISSING_PERMS}  - ${line}"$'\n'
done < <(grep -rn "onCommand" "$SRC_DIR" 2>/dev/null \
    | grep -v "hasPermission" 2>/dev/null \
    | grep -v "public boolean onCommand" 2>/dev/null \
    | grep -v "implements" 2>/dev/null \
    | grep -v "/*" 2>/dev/null \
    | grep -v "*" 2>/dev/null \
    || true)
if [ -n "$MISSING_PERMS" ]; then
    echo -e "${RED}[WARN] Commands without hasPermission():${NC}"
    echo -e "$MISSING_PERMS"
    echo -e "[WARN] Missing permission checks:" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
    echo -e "$MISSING_PERMS" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
    WARN_COUNT=$((WARN_COUNT + 1))
else
    echo -e "${GREEN}  ✓ All commands have permission gates.${NC}"
fi

echo -e "${YELLOW}→ Checking unsafe Java reflection...${NC}"
UNSAFE_REFLECT=$(grep -rn -e "setAccessible(true)" -e "Class.forName" "$SRC_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true)
if [ -n "$UNSAFE_REFLECT" ]; then
    echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN] Raw reflection usage:${NC}"
    echo "$UNSAFE_REFLECT"
    echo -e "[WARN] Unsafe reflection:\n$UNSAFE_REFLECT" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
    WARN_COUNT=$((WARN_COUNT + 1))
else
    echo -e "${GREEN}  ✓ No unsafe reflection.${NC}"
fi

echo -e "${YELLOW}→ Checking Thread.sleep usage...${NC}"
THREAD_SLEEP=$(grep -rn "Thread\.sleep" "$SRC_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true)
if [ -n "$THREAD_SLEEP" ]; then
    echo -e "${RED}[CRITICAL] Thread.sleep detected (blocks main thread):${NC}"
    echo "$THREAD_SLEEP"
    echo -e "[CRITICAL] Thread.sleep:\n$THREAD_SLEEP" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
    WARN_COUNT=$((WARN_COUNT + 1))
else
    echo -e "${GREEN}  ✓ No Thread.sleep found.${NC}"
fi

echo "Warnings: $WARN_COUNT" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo "Step 2: Scan -> OK ($WARN_COUNT warnings)" >> "$REPORT_FILE"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#   STEP 3: BUILD
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
step_header "[3/4] Compiling & reobfuscating production jar..."

./gradlew build
BUILD_EXIT=$?
echo "Step 3: Build -> $([ $BUILD_EXIT -eq 0 ] && echo 'OK' || echo 'FAILED')" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
if [ $BUILD_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
    abort "Build failed!"
fi
echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Build successful.${NC}"
ls -lh build/libs/*.jar 2>/dev/null
ls -lh build/libs/*.jar >> "$REPORT_FILE" 2>/dev/null

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#   STEP 4: CLEAN UP TEMPORARY BUILD ARTIFACTS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
step_header "[4/4] Cleaning up temporary build artifacts..."

for dir in build/tmp build/classes build/generated build/reports build/generated-sources; do
    if [ -d "$dir" ]; then
        rm -rf "$dir"
        echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓ Removed${NC} $dir"
    fi
done

echo "Step 4: Cleanup -> OK" >> "$REPORT_FILE"
echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Temporary artifacts cleaned.${NC}"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#   SUMMARY
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
echo ""
echo -e "${CYAN}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗${NC}"
if [ $WARN_COUNT -gt 0 ]; then
    echo -e "${YELLOW}║   Pipeline finished with $WARN_COUNT warning(s)         ║${NC}"
    echo -e "${YELLOW}║   See: $REPORT_FILE            ║${NC}"
else
    echo -e "${GREEN}║   Pipeline completed — 100% clean                      ║${NC}"
fi
echo -e "${CYAN}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝${NC}"

exit 0

## Changes in this fork

## 2\.0.0 Beta

A beta version of the 2.0.0 update is available on the `gradle` branch, with many changes and improvements. [Try it out!](https://github.com/AlessioDP/libby/tree/gradle)

# Libby

A runtime dependency management library for plugins running in Java-based Minecraft
server platforms.

Libraries can be downloaded from Maven repositories (or direct URLs) into a plugin's data
folder, relocated and then loaded into the plugin's classpath at runtime.
### Why use runtime dependency management?

Due to file size constraints on plugin hosting services like SpigotMC, some plugins with
bundled dependencies become too large to be uploaded.

Using runtime dependency management, dependencies are downloaded and cached by the server
and don't need to be bundled with the plugin, which significantly reduces the size of the
plugin jar.

A smaller plugin jar also means shorter download times and less network strain for authors
who self-host their plugins on servers with limited bandwidth.

### Usage

Firstly, add the maven artifact to your `pom.xml`

```
 
< repository >
  < id >AlessioDP</ id >
  < url >https://repo.alessiodp.com/releases/</ url >
</ repository >

< dependency >
    < groupId >net.byteflux</ groupId >
    < artifactId >libby-bukkit</ artifactId >  
    < version >1.3.2</ version >
</ dependency >
```

Remember to **always** relocate Libby to avoid conflicts

```
< plugin >
    < groupId >org.apache.maven.plugins</ groupId >
< artifactId >maven-shade-plugin</ artifactId >
    < configuration >
        < relocations >
            < relocation >
                < pattern >net.byteflux.libby</ pattern >
                < shadedPattern >yourPackage.libs.net.byteflux.libby</ shadedPattern >
            </ relocation >
        </ relocations >
    </ configuration >
</ plugin >
```

Then, create a new LibraryManager instance

```
// Create a library manager for a Bukkit/Spigot plugin 
 BukkitLibraryManager bukkitLibraryManager = new BukkitLibraryManager ( plugin );

 // Create a library manager for a Bungee plugin 
 BungeeLibraryManager bungeeLibraryManager = new BungeeLibraryManager ( plugin );

 // Also Nukkit, Sponge, and Velocity are supported
```

Create a Library instance with the library builder

```
Library lib = Library . builder ()
    . groupId ( "your{}dependency{}groupId" ) // "{}" is replaced with ".", useful to avoid unwanted changes made by maven-shade-plugin 
    .
artifactId ( "artifactId" )
    . version ( "version" )
     // The following are optional 

     // Sets an id for the library 
    . id ( "my-lib" )
     // Relocation is applied to the downloaded jar before loading it 
    . relocate ( "package{}to{}relocate" , "the{}relocated{}package" ) // "{}" is replaced with ".", useful to avoid unwanted changes made by maven-shade-plugin 
     // The library is loaded into an IsolatedClassLoader, which is in common between every library with the same id 
    . isolatedLoad ( true )
    . classifier ( "customClassifier" )
    . checksum ( "Base64-encoded SHA-256 checksum" )
    . build ();
```

Finally, add Maven Central (or other repositories) to the library manager and download your library. To do this,
you can use the `LibraryManager(Library libraryToLoad)` method, which automatically downloads and then loads the provided library.

```
libraryManager . addMavenCentral ();
 libraryManager . loadLibrary ( lib );
```

Complete code

```
BukkitLibraryManager libraryManager = new BukkitLibraryManager ( plugin );

 Library lib = Library . builder ()
    . groupId ( "your{}dependency{}groupId" ) // "{}" is replaced with ".", useful to avoid unwanted changes made by maven-shade-plugin 
    . artifactId ( "artifactId" )
    . version ( "version" )
     // The following are optional 

     // Sets an id for the library 
    . id ( "my-lib" )
     // Relocation is applied to the downloaded jar before loading it 
    . relocate ( "package{}to{}relocate" , "the{}relocated{}package" ) // "{}" is replaced with ".", useful to avoid unwanted changes made by maven-shade-plugin 
     // The library is loaded into an IsolatedClassLoader, which is in common between every library with the same id 
    . isolatedLoad ( true )
    . classifier ( "customClassifier" )
    . checksum ( "Base64-encoded SHA-256 checksum" )
    . build ();

 libraryManager . addMavenCentral ();
 libraryManager . loadLibrary ( lib );
```

# Citizens2 README

Citizens is an NPC plugin for the Bukkit API. It was first released on March 5, 2011, and has since seen numerous updates. Citizens provides an API which developers can use to create their own NPC characters. More information on the API can be found on the API page of the Citizens Wiki ( <https://wiki.citizensnpcs.co/API> ).

Compatible with:

* Minecraft (for specific compatible version information, see <https://wiki.citizensnpcs.co/Versions> for info)
* CitizensAPI (for compiling purposes only)

# Extra information

Javadoc: <http://jd.citizensnpcs.co>

Spigot page: <https://www.spigotmc.org/resources/citizens.13811>

Developmental builds: <https://ci.citizensnpcs.co/job/Citizens2/>
For questions/help join our discord at: <https://discord.gg/Q6pZGSR>

## Footer"
